title: Home Buying Readiness Quiz - Are You Ready to Buy? | CrunchMilk
metaDescription: Take this free quiz to find out if you're financially ready to buy a home. Assess your down payment, credit, debt, and stability for homeownership.
tagline: Are you truly ready to buy a home, or should you wait?

questions.question: How much do you have saved for a down payment?

options.text: Less than 3% of my target home price
3-5% of my target home price
5-15% of my target home price
20% or more of my target home price

questions.question: What is your current credit score?

options.text: Below 620 or I don't know
740 or higher

questions.question: What is your total debt-to-income ratio (all monthly debts divided by gross income)?

options.text: Over 43% - I have significant debt
36-43% - manageable but tight
28-36% - in decent shape
Under 28% - very little debt

questions.question: How stable is your employment situation?

options.text: I've changed jobs recently or have unstable income
I've been at my job less than a year
Stable employment for 1-2 years
2+ years at current job with reliable income history

questions.question: After a down payment, would you still have an emergency fund?

options.text: No, the down payment would wipe me out
I'd have about 1-2 months of expenses left
I'd have 3-4 months of expenses remaining
I'd still have 6+ months of emergency savings

questions.question: How long do you plan to stay in the area where you'd buy?

options.text: Less than 2 years - I might move soon
2-3 years
3-5 years
5+ years - I'm settled here

questions.question: Have you accounted for the hidden costs of homeownership (maintenance, property tax, insurance, HOA)?

options.text: I haven't thought about costs beyond the mortgage
I know they exist but haven't calculated them
I've estimated most additional costs
I've budgeted 1-3% of home value annually for maintenance plus all extras

questions.question: Have you been pre-approved for a mortgage?

options.text: No, and I haven't talked to any lenders
I've done some online rate checks
I've been pre-qualified but not pre-approved
Yes, I have a pre-approval letter from a lender

scoreRanges.label: Not Ready Yet
scoreRanges.analysis: You're not quite ready to buy a home. Focus on building your down payment, improving your credit score, and reducing existing debt. Renting while you prepare is the smart move right now.

scoreRanges.label: Getting Closer
scoreRanges.analysis: You're making progress toward homeownership but have some gaps to address. Prioritize getting your debt-to-income ratio down and building a larger reserve fund beyond your down payment.

scoreRanges.label: Nearly Ready
scoreRanges.analysis: You're in good shape for homeownership. Take the final steps: get pre-approved, research neighborhoods, and make sure you've budgeted for all the hidden costs of owning a home.

scoreRanges.label: Ready to Buy
scoreRanges.analysis: You're well-prepared to buy a home. You have the financial foundation, stability, and planning needed. Start your home search with confidence and use a calculator to compare renting vs buying in your market.
